name: Chicken Sauce Piquant
recipeInstructions:
1. Brown the chicken in bacon drippings until thoroughly cooked.
2. Prepare the roux using the bacon drippings and flour as directed in Justin Wilson's recipe.
3. Sauté onions, bell pepper, green onions, celery, parsley, and garlic in a separate pan until onions are tender and clear.
4. Combine the sautéed vegetables with the prepared roux to form a smooth paste.
5. Add water to achieve the desired consistency.
6. Incorporate all remaining ingredients (tomato sauce, Chablis wine, stuffed olives, Lea & Perrins, Louisiana hot sauce, dried mint, and salt).
7. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for about 4 to 6 hours until fully cooked.
8. Serve over spaghetti; this recipe serves 20 to 40 people. Leftovers can be frozen.
